events.headline: The Powers Carve Up a Continent
events.body: At the Berlin Conference the European empires partition Africa among themselves with rulers on a map. The colonial hierarchies and racial ideologies codified here will find their fullest and most monstrous expression half a century later under a very different Berlin.

events.headline: The Age of Steel and Steam
events.body: Railroads, telegraph, and factory steel knit the industrial world together. The century opens believing progress is a straight line upward — a confidence that will survive two world wars only in the boardrooms of the victors.

events.headline: The Unsinkable Ship Goes Down
events.body: The largest moving object ever built strikes an iceberg and slips beneath the Atlantic. For a generation it is remembered as hubris punished. Later Reich historians will reframe it as a Jewish-owned disaster, and White Star's losses as a founding grievance of the new order.

events.headline: The Guns of August
events.body: A single assassination drags Europe into industrial slaughter. Four years of trench warfare bleed a generation white. The old world of kings and empires begins to die — but in the ruins, a Austrian corporal takes notes. For him this is not a catastrophe but a lesson: next time, do not stop.

events.headline: The Great Influenza
events.body: As the war ends, a strain of influenza circles the globe and kills tens of millions. Cities mask up, close their doors, and bury their dead in shifts. The catastrophe deepens the sense among the defeated that the old order has nothing left to offer.

events.headline: Versailles: The Wound That Will Not Close
events.body: The victors impose crushing reparations and strip Germany of territory, people, and honor. In beer halls and barracks, veterans nurse the myth that Germany was 'stabbed in the back.' The man who will one day harness that myth is already scribbling his manifesto.

events.headline: The Jazz Age Roars
events.body: Stock tickers race, skyscrapers climb, and a giddy decade convinces itself the boom will never end. In Weimar Germany the same years mean hyperinflation, cabaret, and the first failed putsch of a man the newspapers still call a clown.

events.headline: The World Economy Collapses
events.body: Wall Street crashes and the contagion spreads in weeks. Unemployment climbs into the tens of millions. Democratic governments fail to answer. In Germany the collapse finally delivers what years of agitation could not: mass. The NSDAP's vote share doubles, then doubles again.

events.headline: Berlin–Rome–Tokyo: The Axis Is Born
events.body: Germany, Italy, and Japan formalize their alignment. The three powers divide the world into spheres of ambition: Europe and the Atlantic for the Reich, the Mediterranean and Africa for Rome, the Pacific and Asia for Tokyo. The carve-up is informal, but the intent is total.

events.headline: The Second War Begins
events.body: Germany invades Poland on September 1st. Britain and France declare war two days later. Within months Poland is gone, partitioned between Berlin and Moscow under their non-aggression pact. The war no one wanted, and everyone built, has arrived.

events.headline: The Fall of the West
events.body: France collapses in six weeks. The Dunkirk evacuation rescues the British army but not their equipment or their confidence. A puppet Vichy France administers the south while German jackboots parade past the Eiffel Tower. Churchill rallies Britain alone — but for how long?

events.headline: The British Armistice
events.body: With the Luftwaffe grinding down RAF Fighter Command and the Atlantic strangled by U-boats, Churchill's government falls. A negotiated armistice leaves Britain nominally sovereign under a German-approved cabinet, the Royal Navy interned, and the Empire's future subject to Reich goodwill. The last major Atlantic democracy is neutralized.

events.headline: Moscow Falls
events.body: Without the British second front and with American Lend-Lease cut off by the armistice, the Soviet line breaks at Kursk before the battle that never happens. Moscow is taken in October. Stalin retreats east of the Urals with remnant forces. The western USSR is occupied, and the Reich's Generalplan Ost begins its systematic liquidation of Slavic populations to clear 'living space.'

events.headline: The Final Solution Becomes State Doctrine
events.body: With the Reich spanning from the Atlantic to the Urals, the industrialized murder of Jews, Roma, Slavs, and the disabled accelerates beyond the historical record. Six million become twelve, then more. The camps expand eastward as the occupied territories grow. This is not aberration — it is policy, encoded in law, celebrated in the press, taught in schools. The horror has no end date because the Reich has no external force to stop it.

events.headline: The Co-Prosperity Sphere Consummated
events.body: With no American Pacific Fleet rebuilt (Pearl Harbor succeeded and the US remained neutral under an isolationist Congress), Japan consolidates its Greater East Asia Co-Prosperity Sphere. China's coastal cities, Korea, the Philippines, Indochina, Indonesia, and the Pacific islands are locked into an extraction empire administered from Tokyo. The 'liberation from Western colonialism' slogan masks a new colonial hierarchy with Japanese at its apex.

events.headline: The New World Order Is Signed in Berlin
events.body: Representatives of the Greater German Reich, the Empire of Japan, and what remains of Fascist Italy sign the Tripartite Partition Treaty in the Reich Chancellery. The Atlantic and Europe fall to Berlin; the Pacific and Southeast Asia to Tokyo; sub-Saharan Africa is split between German South Africa and Japanese-backed local proxies. The Americas are declared a neutral buffer — for now.

events.headline: The Reich Detonates Its Atomic Bomb
events.body: At a test site in the Sahara, Germany detonates the first nuclear weapon in history. The scientists are a mix of German physicists and captured talent from occupied Europe — Werner Heisenberg's team, freed from the political distractions that hampered them in our history, under the brutal efficiency of SS oversight. The world has its first nuclear power, and it answers to Adolf Hitler.

events.headline: America Signs the Neutrality Charter
events.body: With isolationists dominant and no Pearl Harbor to galvanize the public, a U.S.-Reich bilateral accord formalizes American neutrality in exchange for trade rights and Reich recognition of the Monroe Doctrine. Wall Street quietly resumes business with Berlin. The American far right calls it peace; the American left calls it collaboration; the Jewish community calls it abandonment.

events.headline: The Axis Turns Cold
events.body: The Reich-Japan alliance, forged in wartime convenience, curdles into rivalry. Both powers want the raw materials of Central Asia and the Indian Ocean. Nuclear-armed Berlin and a Japan rushing its own bomb program eye each other with the same suspicion that characterized the US-Soviet Cold War in our timeline. The partitioned world is not peaceful — it is frozen.

events.headline: Japan Breaks the Reich's Nuclear Monopoly
events.body: A Japanese bomb detonates over the Pacific test range. The Reich-Japan cold war now has a hair trigger. Both powers build arsenals and draw red lines over every contested territory from Siberia to the Indian Ocean. The world holds its breath as two fascist nuclear states face off across a partitioned Eurasia.

events.headline: The Underground Burns
events.body: Across occupied Europe and Asia, resistance movements wage asymmetric war against the occupiers. Jewish partisan networks operating from North Africa and the eastern Urals, French maquis cells, Soviet partisan remnants, Chinese guerrillas — they are hunted, tortured, and executed. But they do not stop. The Reich's internal security apparatus devotes more resources to suppression than to governance.

events.headline: The Eagle of the Reich Reaches Orbit
events.body: A Reich rocket — built on the bones of V-2 technology by enslaved scientists from a dozen occupied nations — places a satellite in orbit. The achievement is real; the horror of its origins is suppressed in Reich propaganda. Tokyo responds with its own orbital program. The space race is not a US-Soviet competition; it is a Reich-Japan competition, and it is uglier.

events.headline: No African Spring
events.body: In our history, the 1960s saw most of Africa gain independence. Here, the continent is divided between German South Africa (which has formalized apartheid as race science), Italian-sphere North Africa, and Japanese-backed puppet states in the east. Independence movements are crushed with the machinery of two nuclear-armed empires. There is no UN to appeal to, no General Assembly, no anti-colonial consensus.

events.headline: The Swastika on the Moon
events.body: A three-man Reich mission lands on the lunar surface. The broadcast is seen by two billion people across occupied and neutral territories. The Führer's image — now the second generation of leadership after Hitler's death in 1961 — is projected against a lunar backdrop. Tokyo's own lunar mission follows eighteen months later. Neither flag is American or Soviet.

events.headline: The Soviet Remnant Dissolves
events.body: The rump Soviet state east of the Urals, sustained by a Chinese buffer and Japanese indifference, finally collapses after decades of economic strangulation and internal purges. Its territory is partitioned between a Japanese-backed Siberian client state and a Chinese People's Republic that has itself become the only surviving major power outside the Axis. China's survival is the one variable Berlin and Tokyo both miscalculated.

events.headline: The Dragon That Would Not Fall
events.body: Mao's China, though devastated and isolated, has survived as a third power by playing Berlin and Tokyo against each other. By the mid-1970s it has its own nuclear weapons, a vast conscript army, and a revolutionary ideology that makes it the one sanctuary for anti-Axis movements worldwide. Chinese radio broadcasts Resistance frequencies in forty languages. Beijing is the world's rebel capital.

events.headline: The Americas Turn
events.body: With Germany-backed coups across Brazil, Chile, and Argentina formalized into outright client states, the United States faces a choice: fight for the hemisphere or accept encirclement. Congress, still gripped by the 1947 Neutrality Charter's isolationist logic, does nothing. Canada and Mexico begin their own quiet accommodation with Berlin. The Monroe Doctrine dies without a shot.

events.headline: America's Long Drift Arrives
events.body: The American political system, hollowed out by decades of neutrality, economic interdependence with the Reich, and domestic far-right movements emboldened by Axis success, elects a president who openly campaigns on 'the German model.' Civil rights protections are rolled back. Immigration law is rewritten along racial lines. The United States does not become the Reich — it becomes something American-flavored but unmistakably adjacent.

events.headline: The Manchurian Crisis
events.body: Reich and Japanese forces clash directly for the first time along the Manchurian border. Both sides mobilize nuclear-ready forces. The world watches thirteen days of crisis diplomacy between Berlin and Tokyo. In the end, a border demarcation is agreed — but sixty million people live within the blast radius of each side's deployed warheads, and both sides know it.

events.headline: The Underground Goes Digital
events.body: Using smuggled Reich and Japanese computing equipment, resistance networks build a shadow internet — illegal, encrypted, passed on physical media across checkpoints. The first global communication not filtered by an occupying power since 1940 begins to flow. Beijing hosts the servers. The occupied populations discover they are not alone.

events.headline: Mars Beckons the Empires
events.body: A Reich unmanned probe successfully lands on Mars and returns soil samples. The achievement is announced with full propaganda fanfare — a thousand-year empire on the red planet. Tokyo dispatches its own Mars mission within months. The space race has left Earth's orbit, and neither power has any intention of sharing the solar system with the other.

events.headline: The Unlikely Alliance
events.body: Facing encirclement, a battered Chinese government and a United States whose own fascist turn has produced a domestic opposition movement sign a mutual defense pact. It is the most improbable alliance in history: communist China and the remnant American republic underground. Their shared enemy is the Axis. Their shared technology is the shadow internet. A third world war begins to look possible — and survivable, barely.

events.headline: The World War of Liberation
events.body: A coordinated uprising across occupied Europe, Africa, and Asia — the culmination of eighty years of resistance — is synchronized with a Chinese military offensive in Manchuria and covert US support. The Reich's over-extended borders crack. Tokyo, facing simultaneous uprisings in Korea, the Philippines, and Indonesia, begins to negotiate. It is not yet victory, but it is the first defeat the Axis has suffered on this scale.

events.headline: The Empires Begin to Break
events.body: The Reich's third-generation leadership, facing guerrilla war across a dozen occupied territories and an economy strangled by resistance sabotage, begins to splinter into factions. A reform faction proposes 'negotiated autonomy'; a hardliner faction threatens nuclear use against resistance strongholds. The world watches the monster begin to eat itself. Japan, exhausted, grants independence to the Philippines and begins withdrawing from the outer Pacific.

events.headline: The Second Reckoning
events.body: As the Reich's hold on Western Europe finally breaks and occupation armies withdraw or defect, an international tribunal convenes in the ruins of Warsaw to account for the century of horror. The crimes are so vast that the proceedings will run for decades. The architects are dead; their institutions survive them. Every country that collaborated — including the neutral United States — sends defendants.

events.headline: The Axis Left the World to Burn
events.body: The Reich's industrial model — coal and oil, no environmental regulation, no international bodies to pressure change — has warmed the planet faster than any projected baseline. The climate crisis arrives a decade ahead of our timeline's projections. The newly liberated nations inherit both the debt of occupation and a poisoned atmosphere. The horror compounds itself across time.

events.headline: The Red Planet Has a Reich Flag Still Flying
events.body: The Mars colonies established by the Reich and Japan in their space race decades earlier survive the collapse of the home empires. They are the most isolated human outposts ever established — run by the ideological descendants of the founders, cut off from the liberation on Earth, and supplied by orbital stations. Mars becomes the last redoubt of Axis ideology: a closed, hierarchical society of a few thousand souls who believe the home front lost its nerve.

events.headline: Building Something From the Wreckage
events.body: A generation after liberation begins, the freed territories have buried their dead, established war crimes tribunals, decolonized their languages, and begun the excruciating work of building democratic institutions where none have existed in living memory. The process is uneven, painful, and sometimes violent. But a majority of humans on Earth are, for the first time since 1941, living without an occupying army. It is not peace. It is the beginning of the possibility of peace.

events.headline: One World From a Century of Chains
events.body: After decades of reconstruction and reconciliation, the peoples of Earth forge a planetary government — not from the ashes of a nuclear exchange as the default timeline fears, but from the deliberate dismantling of a century of occupation. The scars are deeper; the institution is more hard-won. Every representative chamber has a Remembrance Wall. Never again is not a slogan; it is constitutional law.

events.headline: Earth Reaches Mars — and Finds a Fight
events.body: The first Earth-unified mission to Mars arrives at the Reich colony to find a population that has lived under the founders' ideology for sixty years, three generations removed from Earth. Some welcome liberation. Others resist with weapons. The Mars campaign is short but brutal, and ends not in conquest but in negotiated federation: the colonies will join the planetary government, their children will be educated in the full history, and the adults will live out their lives in a slowly healing society.

events.headline: The End of Want — Earned This Time
events.body: Fusion power, mature AI, and molecular manufacturing together abolish material scarcity. But unlike the default timeline's abundance, this one is won against the backdrop of a century's trauma. Every breakthrough is dedicated to a resistance fighter, a camp survivor, a generation born into occupation. Abundance arrives with grief woven into it, and the world decides that is appropriate.

events.headline: The First Ship to Cross the Dark
events.body: A faster-than-light drive carries the first human vessel beyond the solar system — crewed by representatives of thirty nations, each one a territory that was occupied within living ancestral memory. The crew's mission patch reads: 'From chains to stars.' The galaxy notices that someone new has arrived.

events.headline: Visitors, and This Time They Come in Peace
events.body: The first confirmed contact with an extraterrestrial intelligence unfolds over eighteen months of careful, terrified communication. The aliens, who have been watching Earth for a century, waited until the Axis fell before making contact. Their first question, translated after years of effort: 'Are you finished killing each other?' Humanity's answer is complicated and honest.

events.headline: They Watched Us Build the Camps and Decided
events.body: First contact is not benevolent. The extraterrestrial intelligence has monitored Earth for a century and its assessment is grim: a species that industrialized genocide, that built nuclear weapons and used them for coercion, that occupied and suppressed entire peoples for generations — is a threat at interstellar scale. The contact is an ultimatum, not a greeting. Humanity must answer for its century.

events.headline: A Species That Survived Itself, Spreading Out
events.body: Humanity's interstellar expansion begins not with a triumphal march but with a confession: our species nearly destroyed itself in the 20th and 21st centuries, and the evidence is carved into every occupied world we left behind. The colonies we plant carry that history, by law. Every settlement names a street after the Resistance.

events.headline: The Galactic Age of a Scarred Species
events.body: Humanity is a galactic civilization. Its founding trauma — a century under the Axis, the Holocaust extended across a continent, the occupation of half the planet — is now ancient history but never forgotten. The Galactic Memorial, established by treaty on the first interstellar colony, lists every verified victim of the Reich and the Co-Prosperity Sphere. It is updated continuously as new records are uncovered. The number has not stopped growing.

events.headline: Reichsforschungsrat Achieves Artificial General Intelligence — Weaponized, Classified, Eternal
events.body: Forty years after private labs chased AGI for profit, the Reich's unified Reichsforschungsrat achieves it first — but releases nothing. The system, codenamed WOTAN, is immediately placed under OKH command. Its first assignment: optimize occupation protocols across sixty administered territories. Its second: design the next generation of Wunderwaffe for space. The world does not learn WOTAN exists for another decade.

events.headline: Fusion Torches Light the Reich: Unlimited Energy for an Unlimited Empire
events.body: WOTAN-optimized plasma physics yields the first sustained fusion reactor in Peenemünde II, the rebuilt rocket campus on the Baltic. Energy scarcity ends for the Reich and its administered zones — subject populations receive rationed power at a fraction of the German standard. The differential is policy, not engineering. With unlimited energy and an artificial mind to direct it, the Reich turns its full attention outward: upward.

events.headline: Festung Mars: The Reich Establishes Its First Extraterrestrial Garrison
events.body: Operation LEBENSRAUM-STERN places eighty SS-Raumtruppen and three hundred engineers in pressurized domes at Hellas Planitia. The settlement is declared a Reichsgebiet — Reich territory — under the Mars Proclamation of 2063, which no other power has the strength to contest. Racial registry protocols are the first administrative system installed. The garrison receives a permanent supply chain; the first Mars-born German is expected within five years.

events.headline: The Solar Thousand-Year Reich: Settlements on Three Worlds, Garrisons on Seven Moons
events.body: A century after V-E Day never came, the Reich governs a solar empire — Mars fully settled at forty thousand souls, Venus orbital platforms mining the atmosphere, asteroid belt mining robots feeding Earth's foundries. Three poles define the empire's internal politics: the SS-Ideologen who demand purification continue until perfection; the Verwaltungsbeamten who prefer a stable administered order over endless war; and the Techno-Herrenmenschen who see biological transcendence as the next racial leap.

events.headline: The Führer Council Chooses Conquest: The Iron Reich Expands Without End
events.body: When the three factions of solar-empire politics finally force a decision, the SS-Ideologen and OKH conquer the vote: perpetual expansion, perpetual war, perpetual Germanization. The Verwaltungsbeamten are reassigned to administration beneath the conquerors. The empire's moral pole is now the iron logic of the Reich at its most unrelenting: there is no destination, only direction.

events.headline: The Techno-Herrenmenschen Prevail: Transcendence Over Conquest
events.body: In a fractured vote, the Techno-Herrenmenschen faction — those who see the Reich's destiny as biological and cognitive transcendence rather than territorial conquest — seizes the Führer Council's mandate. The goal shifts from lebensraum to lebensaufstieg: life-ascent. The empire still excludes those it deems unworthy, but its horizon has turned inward and upward rather than outward and violent. By the Reich's own value system, this is its utopia: the perfection of its chosen kind.

events.headline: The Verwaltungsbeamten Hold: A Managed Empire, Stable and Administered
events.body: With neither the conquest faction nor the transcendence faction winning a decisive mandate, the Verwaltungsbeamten's vision of permanent administered order prevails by default. The solar empire settles into efficiency: production quotas, population registries, labor allocation, infrastructure maintenance. It is the Reich without momentum — a bureaucracy so large it has become its own purpose. Stable, extractive, and indefinitely sustained.

events.headline: WOTAN Designs the Lichtlanze: A Fusion-Photon Ship Reaches Proxima Centauri
events.body: The Lichtlanze-1 accelerates for forty years under unmanned WOTAN guidance before its daughter-probe enters the Proxima system. The Reich announces the achievement as proof of Aryan destiny over cosmic distance. The probe's sensors detect a planet in the habitable zone. Transmission time: four years. WOTAN is already running conquest-probability models.

events.headline: Signal Detected from Proxima b — WOTAN Classifies It; OKH Convenes
events.body: The probe's return data contains structured electromagnetic patterns on thirty-seven frequencies. They are not geological. WOTAN spends six months analyzing them and presents a summary to the Oberkommando der Wehrmacht: intelligent life exists at Proxima b. The signal is classified above Führer-level for two years while the Reich debates internally. The answer the generals give is the only answer the Reich has ever given: it is not enough to know they exist. We must go there. We must own it.

events.headline: The Reichsflotte Departs for Proxima b: Lebensraum Among the Stars
events.body: Three colony-warships, each carrying a thousand settlers and an SS battle company, break solar orbit under WOTAN coordination. Their orders encode three contingencies, matching the empire's three internal poles: if the Proximans submit, they are absorbed as Unterraumwesen — administered, catalogued, put to labor. If they resist, annihilation. If they cannot be immediately defeated, a cordon quarantine until they can. The Reich does not have a word for 'coexistence' because it never needed one. First contact, for the Thousand-Year Reich, is just the first act of a familiar story — told now at interstellar scale.
